title: Hypericum xylosteifolium
taxonomy: Hypericum xylosteifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae. It belongs to the genus Hypericum, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as St. John's Wort. The classification of Hypericum xylosteifolium places it within the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This species is native to Europe and parts of Western Asia, and is characterized by its small, yellow flowers and oval-shaped leaves. Further classification includes its rank as a species, which is a taxonomic rank below that of genus.

intro: Hypericum xylosteifolium, also known as St. John's Wort of the Andes or Peruvian St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant species within the St. John's Wort genus, Hypericum. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, depression, and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Hypericum xylosteifolium include hyperforin, hypericin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Hypericum xylosteifolium is often used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are commonly used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
